item.title: Tycoon Plows Billions Into EVs as His Vietnam Stock Booms 1,000%
item.description: Vietnam’s richest person spent billions of dollars of his personal fortune on his emerging electric automaker and other ventures last year as the shares of his corporate group soared.

item.title: Tuas’ $1.1 Billion M1 Deal Lapsed Amid Singapore Regulator Probe
item.description: Tuas Ltd.’s planned $1.1 billion acquisition of Singapore major telecom operator M1 Ltd. has collapsed days after regulators halted their review of the transaction.

item.title: China’s National Team Set to Cut ETF Stakes by 90% in First Half
item.description: China’s so-called National Team is poised to cut its holdings of exchange-traded funds that track domestic equities by about 90% during the first half of 2026, according to an analysis by Bloomberg Intelligence.

item.title: Nidec CEO Says Data Center Demand Cushioning Blow From Scandals
item.description: Nidec Corp.’s recent financial performance is being bolstered by its data-center business, despite quality control and accounting problems at the supplier of electric motors, Chief Executive Officer Mitsuya Kishida said.

item.title: North Carolina Sues VinFast Over Delayed, Downsized Factory Plan
item.description: North Carolina sued Vietnamese electric vehicle maker VinFast Auto Ltd., alleging the company breached agreements tied to a planned electric vehicle and battery manufacturing facility, and seeking to reclaim the project site.

item.title: Yen Traders Brace for Intervention Risk as Monday Holidays Loom
item.description: Yen traders are on alert for the risk of intervention on Monday as holidays from London to New York reduce market liquidity.

item.title: Yield Curves May Steepen Further in Key Southeast Asian Markets
item.description: The yield gap between short- and long-dated bonds in several Southeast Asian countries may widen further, as elevated oil prices increase inflationary pressures and deepen fiscal strains.

item.title: Japan Inflation Eases to Four-Year Low, Complicating BOJ Hike
item.description: Japan’s key inflation gauge rose at the slowest pace in four years as the government continued to help ease the cost of living, creating difficult optics for the Bank of Japan to raise interest rates soon.

item.title: Investors Look Beyond TSMC as AI Boom Spreads to New Winners
item.description: After several years flying high as Asia’s best Nvidia Corp. proxy, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. is increasingly vying with other artificial intelligence stocks for investor attention.

item.title: Ross Raises Forecasts Amid Sales Surge, Record Same-Store Growth
item.description: Ross Stores Inc. raised its sales and profit guidance after first-quarter results surpassed consensus estimates, aided by strong customer traffic among younger shoppers and the highest same-store growth in the company’s history.

item.title: Only High-Earning Britons Are Defying the Energy Shock, GfK Says
item.description: Confidence among the lowest-earning Britons plunged in May and even those on average pay are digging into savings to meet everyday expenses as the Iran energy shock hits household budgets, a key survey found.

item.title: Zhipu, Minimax Seen Joining HK Tech Gauge, Luring More AI Bets
item.description: China’s hottest AI stocks may be among candidates for inclusion in Hong Kong stock gauges, opening access to trading links that may trigger billions of dollars in inflows.

item.title: Gold Steady as US-Iran Signals Keep Rate Hike Bets Simmering
item.description: Gold moved in a narrow range as conflicting signals on the progress of US-Iran ceasefire talks continued to keep traders guessing over whether central banks may need to keep interest rates higher for longer to combat inflation.

item.title: IMF Approves About $1 Billion Disbursement for Argentina in Fresh Win for Milei
item.description: The International Monetary Fund’s executive board approved Argentina’s latest program review to unlock about $1 billion, a vote of confidence in President Javier Milei despite missing a key program target.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Rise on Optimism Over Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian equities were set to open higher Friday, as optimism around ongoing US-Iran talks boosted US stocks and weighed on crude prices.

item.title: Oil Rises After Three-Day Drop With Iran-US Talks in Focus
item.description: Oil rose after three days of declines, as statements by Iran on uranium and the Strait of Hormuz pared earlier optimism over progress in the negotiations with the US.

item.title: What to Know About the SpaceX IPO
item.description: Elon Musk gave the financial world a lot to ponder as SpaceX made public its plans for an initial public offering. The IPO is poised to be the largest stock-market debut in history, but as financial paperwork unveiled on May 20 confirmed, SpaceX’s success as a public enterprise will depend on its ability to meet lofty goals across space exploration, satellite communications and artificial intelligence.

item.title: Workday Rallies After Results Quiet Fears of AI Disruption
item.description: Workday Inc., a maker of workplace management software, posted better-than-expected results in the first quarter, helping quell concerns that the company will be disrupted by artificial intelligence.

item.title: UBS Hits Back Over Nazi-Accounts Probe Amid Senate Pressure
item.description: UBS Group AG accused the US lawyer overseeing a six-year-old inquiry into Credit Suisse’s handling of Nazi-linked accounts of bias and exceeding his mandate, deepening a standoff between the Swiss bank and its critics in Washington.

item.title: Cursor Hits $3 Billion Annual Sales Rate Ahead of SpaceX Deal
item.description: Cursor’s annualized revenue hit $3 billion in late April, according to a person familiar with the matter, indicating growing demand for the company’s AI coding software ahead of an expected deal to be acquired by Elon Musk’s SpaceX. The artificial intelligence startup’s annualized revenue, which projects sales for a 12-month-period based on recent performance, topped $2 billion in February, Bloomberg previously reported. Cursor now has more than 3,000 customers paying at least $100,000 each for

item.title: $50 Trillion Safe-Haven Debt Market Upended by Iran War Inflation
item.description: Investors in the safe haven of G7 debt want protection as the conflict in the Middle East threatens another bout of inflation.

item.title: Flight Firm CAE Says Defense Deals, Cost Cutting to Drive Profit Surge
item.description: Flight simulator firm CAE Inc. unveiled a plan to increase operating income by 30% or more by 2030 as its new chief executive officer embarks on a strategy that includes a greater focus on defense clients.

item.title: Zoom Sales Forecast Tops Estimates After Product Expansion
item.description: Zoom Communications Inc. projected stronger-than-anticipated sales growth, suggesting that customers are adopting its expanded suite of office products.

item.title: Take-Two Confirms November Debut for ‘Grand Theft Auto VI’
item.description: Take-Two Interactive Software Inc. confirmed a Nov. 19 release date for Grand Theft Auto VI, expected to be one of the biggest video-game titles ever.

item.title: Bird Construction Readies Its First Corporate Bond Sale
item.description: Bird Construction, a Canadian building firm recently named the lead construction partner for a data center in Saskatchewan, is looking to sell bonds for the first time, and aims to raise around C$250 million ($181 million), according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Netflix, Spotify to Face Higher Costs as Canada Changes Rules
item.description: Canada will require streaming giants such as Netflix Inc. and Spotify Technology SA to spend 15% of their domestic annual revenues on Canadian content, moving ahead with a law that’s been identified as a trade irritant by the US Trade Representative.

item.title: JPMorgan Boosts Warner Bros. Loan to $10 Billion as Merger Looms
item.description: A JPMorgan Chase & Co.-led bank group boosted a leveraged loan offering for Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. to $10.2 billion as the media company looks to refinance short-term debt ahead of its planned acquisition by Paramount Skydance Corp.

item.title: Canada Pension Boss Warns on AI-Fueled Valuations as Stocks Keep Rising
item.description: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board’s top executive said the firm is getting increasingly uncomfortable with rich valuations in a stock market that’s dominated by technology and artificial intelligence companies.

item.title: Aquarian Seeks More Backers for $4.1 Billion Brighthouse Deal
item.description: Aquarian Holdings has approached prospective investors to take a piece of the roughly $3 billion committed by Mubadala Capital to support its acquisition of insurer Brighthouse Financial Inc.

item.title: The AI-Voice Startup Angling to Give Audiobooks Their Viral Moment
item.description: ElevenLabs is taking on Spotify and Audible in a looming battle over becoming the go-to platform for audiobooks
